member.office_address: 143 Cannon House Office Building Washington DC 20515-3507

form_analysis.form_header_text: Rep. Max Miller Fiscal Year 2027 Appropriations Programmatic and Language Request Form
form_analysis.form_instructions_text: Thank you for your interest in submitting an appropriations request to my congressional office for Fiscal Year 2027 (FY27). Below you will find a copy of the submission form for programmatic and language requests for FY27 that my office will use this year. Please take a moment to read the instructions carefully. It is important to note that the House Committee on Appropriations will only consider requests for programmatic increases or decreases. In order to provide the information required of me by the House Appropriations Committee, I have developed the below application form for your use. Please note that you must fill out the entire application. The Committee requires all information for submission. Failing to answer all the questions may jeopardize your request. As noted on the form, please provide us with the President's budget request level (if it is available at the time of submission or previous year's PBR). If you are submitting multiple requests, you must provide an overall priority ranking for each request. Please fill out separate requests for language and programmatic funding. You do not need to fill out both sections for each request. Please keep answers concise and to a maximum of 1-2 paragraphs. Incomplete submissions will not be accepted. If you have questions, please do not hesitate to contact my office at mmillerapprops@mail.house.gov 202-225-3876. Please note that Community Project Funding (CPF) request forms will be available once FY27 guidelines are released.

sections.description: Information about the organization and individual submitting the request.
Details regarding the specific request and its impact.

fields.label: Please give a short title to your request.
fields.rationale: This is the standard short title for the request as provided in the reference values.

fields.label: Please give a brief description of the request.
fields.draft_value:
* $9,000,000,000 for NASA's Science Mission Directorate (SMD)
* Supports mission-critical work across Earth Science, Planetary Science, Astrophysics, Heliophysics, and Biological and Physical Sciences
* Ensures sustained investment in a diverse portfolio of robotic exploration and scientific research missions
* Aligns with the $1.75 billion increase over FY2026 enacted levels to restore program stability
fields.rationale: This value matches the pre-computed funding ask and the programmatic request context provided for NASA SMD.

fields.label: If your organization is submitting more than one request, please list the priority of this request (answer "1" if you are only submitting one request).
fields.rationale: The Planetary Society's primary advocacy focus for this appropriations cycle is the topline funding for NASA's Science Mission Directorate.

fields.label: Please describe the problem or issue your request will address.
fields.draft_value:
* Addresses workforce instability following significant budget fluctuations and proposed cuts in FY 2026
* Counters rapid expansion of Chinese space science ambitions, including lunar sample returns and planned missions to Jupiter
* Prevents the loss of decades of specialized technical capability within the U.S. aerospace workforce and contractor base
* Resolves funding shortfalls that threaten the schedule and viability of peer-reviewed Decadal Survey mission priorities
fields.rationale: Draws on advocacy talking points regarding China competition, workforce stability, and the impact of the FY 2026 budget cycle.

fields.label: Please describe the goals, results, and/or expected outcome of the request.
fields.draft_value:
* Maintains U.S. global leadership in space through the successful execution of rigorously vetted National Academies Decadal Survey priorities
* Generates approximately $3 in economic return for every $1 invested, based on NASA economic impact data
* Secures the STEM talent pipeline by providing inspiration and research opportunities for the next generation of professionals
* Delivers transformative scientific discoveries and practical applications in weather forecasting and planetary defense
fields.rationale: Based on advocacy talking points regarding U.S. leadership, economic impact, and scientific roadmaps.

fields.label: Please tell me how this request will support the constituents of OH-07.
fields.draft_value:
* Supports a local space science economy that received over $28.3 million in NASA obligations between FY2023 and FY2025
* Directly benefits OH-07 employers like MSM Group Inc in Cleveland ($5.6M award) and Zin Technologies, Inc. ($63M+ in contracts)
* Funds the Ohio Aerospace Institute in Brookpark for the Here to Observe (H2O) project to engage regional space consortia
* Sustains high-skill jobs in Cleveland and Brookpark while providing site-wide access to advanced computational tools for local industry
fields.rationale: Utilizes specific district spending data including total obligations and local recipient names (MSM Group, Zin Technologies, Ohio Aerospace Institute).

fields.label: Please tell me why any federal resources used to carry out this programmatic/language request would be an appropriate, constitutional use of taxpayer resources.
fields.draft_value:
* Executes fundamental public responsibilities for space science and exploration codified in Title 51 of the U.S. Code
* Advances national security, economic competitiveness, and scientific leadership in ways no single private entity can replicate
* Promotes the general welfare through essential data for weather forecasting, disaster response, and planetary defense
* Enhances national prestige and strategic positioning in the 21st-century space race with international competitors
fields.rationale: References Title 51 of the U.S. Code and advocacy points on public responsibility and national competitiveness.

fields.label: Please list the other House members you are submitting your request to.
fields.draft_value:
* Rep. Don Bacon (R-NE)
* Rep. Judy Chu (D-CA)
fields.rationale: References the House members identified as leading the existing support letter in the provided context.

fields.label: Please list any Senators you are submitting your request to.
fields.rationale: The provided context states that a letter does not yet exist for the Senate side.
